assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 63-71 detail each criterion individually as PASS with code-level evidence. All five criterion-N.md files confirm PASS verdicts: criterion-1.md (single license filter), criterion-2.md (comma-separated filter), criterion-3.md (400 validation), criterion-4.md (pagination integration), criterion-5.md (response shape unchanged).

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task spec exactly: 2 modified + 1 created, no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Lines 27-29 list the exact three files: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (created).

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items exist on this PR'. No subtask-N.md files exist in the outputs directory (only report.md and criterion-1 through criterion-5.md are present).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are verification report files. No .rs, .toml, or other code files were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents an 'Overall: PASS' verdict with detailed findings across all domains. No merge action, merge command, or auto-merge instruction appears anywhere in the report. The report ends with a generated-by footer, not a merge operation.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across 3 files'. Lines 45-53 detail the specific patterns scanned: hardcoded passwords, API keys (AKIA, sk-, ghp_, xoxb-), private keys (BEGIN.*PRIVATE KEY), environment files, cloud credentials, database credentials.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/package.rs is a new test file with 4 test functions and 10+ assertions'. Lines 106-108 confirm: 'tests/api/package.rs is a new file (listed in the task's "Files to Create" section and confirmed by the new file mode 100644 header in the diff)... Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains four domain sections: 'Intent Alignment' (line 21) with Scope Containment (line 24), Diff Size (line 33), Commit Traceability (line 37); 'Security' (line 41) with Sensitive Pattern Scan (line 43); 'Correctness' (line 55) with CI Status (line 57), Acceptance Criteria (line 60), Verification Commands (line 73); 'Style/Conventions' (line 77) with Repetitive Test Detection (line 83), Test Documentation (line 94), Eval Quality (line 102), Test Change Classification (line 106). Test Quality summary row (line 13) combines: 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 26-39): file-by-file scope comparison listing each of the 3 files with their role. Security (lines 45-53): lists 6 specific pattern categories scanned (passwords, API keys with prefix examples, private keys, env files, cloud credentials, database credentials). Correctness (lines 62-71): per-criterion verification citing specific functions (validate_license_param, spdx::Expression::parse, Condition::any, is_in), test names, and assertions. Style/Conventions (lines 84-108): per-test-function quality assessment naming each of the 4 tests with their distinct scenarios and doc comment text.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews exist)'. Lines 102-104: 'Eval Quality -- N/A: No eval result reviews found on this PR. No eval quality metrics to assess.' The Test Quality combination is PASS, not affected by Eval Quality.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md lines 102-104). No subtask-N.md files exist in the outputs directory. The only files are report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. Root-Cause Investigation is also N/A (line 6: 'No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'), confirming no sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items exist on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. Both appear in the summary table (lines 4-16) as top-level orchestrator verdicts, positioned before the domain-specific detailed sections (Intent Alignment starts at line 21).
